question.text: The PFRDA has the power to investigate matters where:

answer.text: Only when directed by the Supreme Court
The PFRDA believes activities of a pension fund or intermediary are inimical to subscriber interests
Only when a formal complaint is filed by a subscriber
Only at the direction of the Ministry of Finance

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The PFRDA Act gives the authority power to initiate investigation on its own if it believes that activities of a pension fund or actions of an intermediary are detrimental to subscribers' interests. **The Trap:** PFRDA can initiate suo motu investigations — it does not need a complaint or court direction. This is similar to SEBI's investigation powers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Investigation powers of regulators are tested across NISM exams. SEBI has similar powers under the SEBI Act. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** PFRDA also has search and seizure powers to investigate contraventions of the Act. Maintain complete and accurate records at all times.
